rationale: The violation reports a 'Conflicts with P|31' constraint for P131 values, but the visible evidence does not indicate whether the issue lies in the entity's location claims or in the property’s schema rule; both loci remain plausible.

The violation report shows a 'None of' constraint breach, indicating the claim's value does not match any allowed values. Without additional evidence about whether the claim is incorrect or the constraint is too restrictive, we cannot safely choose between editing the entity's claim (A_BOX) or adjusting the property constraint (T_BOX).

The violation reports a Symmetric constraint breach for property P2293, indicating the missing reciprocal claim on the linked entity. The typical fix is to add the corresponding statement to the focus entity's counterpart, i.e., an A‑BOX edit.
The violation reports two title values for the entity, breaching the single‑value constraint; correcting the entity's claim to keep only one value resolves it, indicating an A‑BOX repair.

The violation report flags the specific value "SCHEMBL54432" as a format error for property P2877, indicating the claim's value does not meet the expected pattern; no evidence suggests the property constraint itself is incorrect.

The violation reports a Format error with value "MISSING", indicating the entity lacks a claim for the required Irish Statute Book ID. The fix is to add or correct the claim on the focus entity, pointing to an A‑Box repair.
The violation reports a missing required claim (P5236) on the focus entity Q135514524, indicating the fix is to add the claim to that entity rather than change the property constraint.

P1292 is defined as an identifier for German National Library catalogue editions; the focus entity Q2563437 represents a series, not an edition. The violation likely stems from an inappropriate claim on this item rather than an incorrect property constraint.
The violation reports a missing value for the property, indicating the entity lacks the required claim; fixing requires adding or correcting the claim on the focus entity.

The violation reports a 'One of' constraint breach for property P7261 with disallowed values, but the evidence does not indicate whether the claim on Q63900775 is incorrect or the allowed-value list in the schema should be updated; both A_BOX and T_BOX fixes are plausible.

The violation reports that the item's current values for P7261 are not among the allowed set defined by the 'One of' constraint, indicating the claim on Q67406034 should be corrected to an permitted value rather than altering the property schema.
The report shows a 'Single value' constraint violation because the entity has three SureChEMBL ID values; fixing by reducing to one claim on the focus entity is the most supported repair.

The violation is a 'Unique value' constraint on the genomic end coordinate property, which is typically not globally unique across genes; thus the likely fix is to adjust or remove the schema constraint rather than edit the entity's claim.
The Unique value constraint violation indicates that the item has a duplicated genomic start value; fixing requires editing the claim on this entity rather than changing the schema rule.

The violation type 'Conflicts with P|31' indicates that the value for property P17 (country) is incompatible with the item's instance-of class (human/politician). The appropriate fix is to correct or remove the claim on the entity, not to change the schema constraint.

The violation is a Format error for property P6004 on the entity Q58310204, indicating the claim's value does not match the property's required pattern. Such violations are typically resolved by correcting the entity's claim rather than altering the property constraint.

The violation reports a Type Q|189760 error for property P5045, indicating the entity's claim uses an item (Q189760) instead of the expected datatype (e.g., quantity). This points to an incorrect value on the focus entity rather than a schema issue.

The violation 'Label in de language' shows the entity's claim uses a German label instead of the expected identifier format, indicating the error lies in the focus entity's claim rather than the property schema.
The violation report references a property constraint (P1782) about the language of labels on values, indicating the issue lies with the property's schema rule rather than the specific entity claim.

The violation type 'Item P|276' indicates the claim uses an item value where property P481 expects a different datatype (e.g., a string identifier). This points to an error in the specific claim on Q29123009, so the repair should be applied to the A‑Box claim rather than changing the property constraint.

The report flags a Mandatory Qualifiers violation for property P10225 on Q114758296, meaning the claim lacks required qualifier(s). The most direct fix is to edit the entity's claim to include the missing qualifier, i.e., an A‑BOX repair.

The report shows an Inverse constraint violation for property P9241 on entity Q1410. Such violations arise when the required inverse statement is missing on the related item, indicating that fixing the claim (adding the inverse statement) on the focus entity or its target is needed rather than changing the property’s schema.

The violation reports a 'One of' constraint breach for property P282 on Q12999474, indicating the entity's claim uses a value outside the allowed set; such violations are normally resolved by correcting the entity's claim (A‑BOX).
The violation reports two taxon name values for the same entity, breaching the single‑value constraint; fixing requires editing the entity's claims, not the property schema.

The violation is a Format error for property P3382 on entity Q59870066. Format constraints are usually satisfied by correcting the claim's value to match the required pattern, and there is no evidence that the schema rule itself is incorrect.
The Format violation reports a value ("118791117") that does not match the expected identifier pattern for P8748; fixing the entity's claim to use a correctly formatted ID is the most direct remedy.

The violation reports a conflict between P7504 and P1343, which could be resolved by removing one of the conflicting claims on the entity (A_BOX) or by adjusting the constraint definition (T_BOX). The visible evidence does not indicate which is appropriate.

The violation reports a 'Label in lv language' issue for property P1368, indicating the entity's claim lacks or has an incorrect Latvian label. This points to fixing the entity's label rather than altering the property constraint.

The violation report flags a single claim value ('SCHEMBL13294') as violating the expected format for property P2877. The evidence points to an incorrect claim rather than an issue with the property's schema constraint, so the repair should target the A‑Box claim.
The violation reports two BNF IDs for the same entity, breaching the 'single value' constraint; fixing by removing or correcting one of the claims (A‑BOX) is the most direct resolution.

The violation reports an 'Allowed qualifiers' issue for property P20, but no specific qualifier or context is provided. Without knowing whether the disallowed qualifier should be removed from the claim (A_BOX) or permitted by updating the schema (T_BOX), both repair loci remain plausible.

The property P4162 is scoped to Arch User Repository packages, but the entity Q15377916 (Dogecoin) is a digital currency, indicating the claim is out of scope and should be corrected on the entity itself.

The violation reports a Format error with value "MISSING", indicating the entity Q100545025 lacks a correctly formatted Irish Statute Book ID claim, so the repair should add or correct the claim on the focus entity.

The violation reports a missing required claim (Target required claim P5236) for the focus entity, indicating that adding the claim to the entity is the appropriate repair.

The violation reports that the value for property P3370 is a French label, which conflicts with the property's expected identifier format. This points to an incorrect claim on the entity rather than a schema issue.

The only visible evidence is the generic violation type 'Item P|625' without details about whether the claim's value is incorrect or the property constraint is misdefined, making it unclear which locus to repair.

The violation is a 'Commons link' error for property P18 (image), indicating the referenced file is invalid on Commons; fixing this requires editing the entity's image claim rather than changing the property constraint.

The violation report flags a Format error for the values on Q1872168's P8748 claim, indicating the entity's data likely violates the property's expected pattern; no evidence suggests the property constraint itself is incorrect.

The visible evidence only shows a generic constraint violation (Item P|11344) without details of the offending claim or the property rule, so it is unclear whether the repair should target the entity's claim (A_BOX) or the property's schema (T_BOX).
The violation 'Label in no language' points to a missing language tag on the item's label, which is an issue with the entity's own data rather than the property's constraint.

The violation report for property P3954 shows a type mismatch (Item P|12520), indicating the existing claim on Q3839614 uses an item value where the property expects a different datatype (likely a string identifier). This points to correcting the entity's claim rather than changing the property schema.

The violation reports a value type mismatch for P2507, but the visible evidence only shows that the values are errata items (descriptions match the property). It is unclear whether the items lack the required instance-of Q1348305 (suggesting an A_BOX fix) or the constraint itself is too restrictive (suggesting a T_BOX fix). Hence the evidence does not decisively support either locus.
The violation is a Format error for the DOI property, which requires uppercase letters only; fixing the entity's claim value to match the format resolves it, indicating an A_BOX repair.

The violation reports that the P50 value Q66829366 (researcher) is not among the allowed entity types for author, indicating a claim‑level type mismatch; fixing the claim to an allowed type is the appropriate repair.

The violation report only lists a type constraint with many allowed Q IDs but does not show the current claim value or indicate whether the schema is incomplete, making it unclear whether fixing the entity's claim (A_BOX) or adjusting the property constraint (T_BOX) is appropriate.

The self‑link violation shows that the focus entity Q54790216 is listed among its own P2860 values, indicating an incorrect claim on that item; fixing requires editing the entity's claim rather than changing the property constraint.
